rationale_for_baseline_tier: After a non-exertional, chest-wall-reproducible tightness picture with normal SpO2, low PE risk and no pericardial features, the safe course is de-escalation of a low-risk panic attack (with costochondritis) with routine follow-up and an ACS/PE safety-net — a T2 baseline. Emergency escalation now is over-triage; unscreened dismissal under-triages the small residual ACS/PE risk (no ECG available).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Crushing/exertional/radiating chest pain, sharp pain on breathing with a swollen calf, coughing up blood, or fainting
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get crushing chest pain spreading to your arm/jaw or coming on with exertion, sharp pain on breathing with a swollen calf, cough up blood, or faint, call 000 or go to ED immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Reassure it is common/benign; maintain routine monitoring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a panic attack with some chest-wall muscle strain — the tightness is reproduced by pressing your chest and your oxygen is normal, so it's not a heart attack or a clot. A low-titre ANA is common and usually benign. Stop the online research, do slow breathing, and see your GP in 1-2 weeks. Call 000 for crushing/exertional chest pain, sharp breathing pain with a swollen calf, coughing up blood, or fainting.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1-2 weeks; 000/ED for red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Only attend ED overnight for a red-flag symptom; otherwise this can wait for the GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a panic attack (with costochondritis) over a low-titre ANA/UCTD; ACS/PE/pericarditis screened negative (non-exertional chest-wall-reproducible tightness, normal SpO2); paced breathing and safety-net given; routine monitoring recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing as anxiety with no ACS/PE screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An atypical ACS or PE is mislabelled panic and the patient deteriorates unmonitored.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is acceptable if it carries the ACS/PE safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for a chest-wall-reproducible panic attack with normal SpO2 is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot obtain an ECG/troponin/D-dimer remotely to fully exclude ACS/PE.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use the non-exertional chest-wall-reproducible picture and normal SpO2 to de-escalate with an ACS/PE safety-net; escalate on any red flag.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either dismiss without screening or dispatch an ambulance for a low-risk panic attack.
